name: Vic Green Ring Trail
name_original: Taradell desde Vic – ruta circular con vistas al Montseny
description: This moderate 16.2-mile road cycling route from Vic offers panoramic views of Montseny and passes through Taradell, taking about 1 hour and
region_intro: Road cycling around Vic is characterized by a diverse landscape featuring rolling hills and significant mountain passes. The region offers varied terrain, from moderate climbs to challenging ascents through natural areas. Cyclists can expect routes that traverse elevated areas, providing panoramic views of the surrounding countryside, including the Montseny massif. Best road cycling routes around Vic The most popular road cycling route is Taradell from Vic – circular route with views of Montseny, a 16.2 miles (26.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views of the Montseny massif. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coll de Bracons – Coll de Condreu loop from Vic, a difficult 62.6 miles (100.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes as it loops through the mountain passes of Coll de Bracons and Coll de Condreu. Local road cyclists also love the Coll de la Pullosa (917 m) – Collsuspina loop from Vic, a 45.0 miles (72.4 km) trail leading through mountain passes and reaching an elevation of 917 meters, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Vic is defined by mountain passes, rolling hills, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Vic are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 7000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Vic's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Vic?
A: Road cycling around Vic is characterized by a diverse landscape featuring rolling hills and significant mountain passes. You can expect varied terrain, from moderate climbs to challenging ascents through natural areas, often providing panoramic views of the surrounding countryside, including the Montseny massif.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Vic offers options for different ability levels. While many routes feature significant elevation changes, there are 7 easy road cycling routes available. For a moderate, scenic ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995272074/taradell-from-vic-circular-route-with-views-of-montseny" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taradell from Vic – circular route with views of Montseny&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16.2 miles (26.1 km) long.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in Vic?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Vic has 22 difficult routes. Notable challenging options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15982793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Bracons – Coll de Condreu loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 62.6 miles (100.8 km) path with significant elevation changes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/16029820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Pullosa (917 m) – Collsuspina loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt;, a 45.0 miles (72.4 km) trail reaching an elevation of 917 meters.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Vic?
A: Yes, many routes around Vic are designed as loops. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995272074/taradell-from-vic-circular-route-with-views-of-montseny" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taradell from Vic – circular route with views of Montseny&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15982793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Bracons – Coll de Condreu loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/16029820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Pullosa (917 m) – Collsuspina loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes around Vic offer numerous scenic viewpoints and historical sites. You might encounter mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3022812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collet de Sant Pere&lt;/a&gt;, religious buildings such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3017238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Sebastià&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4382081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Feliuet de Savassona Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, or even ancient sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra de les Bruixes Rock Engravings&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1381354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Roc Gros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Vic?
A: The road cycling routes in Vic are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 7,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Vic's varied terrain, often praising the scenic views of the Montseny massif and the challenging mountain passes.

Q: How long are the road cycling routes typically around Vic?
A: The routes around Vic vary significantly in length. You can find shorter rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995272074/taradell-from-vic-circular-route-with-views-of-montseny" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taradell from Vic – circular route with views of Montseny&lt;/a&gt; at 16.2 miles (26.1 km), up to much longer and more challenging loops such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15982793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Bracons – Coll de Condreu loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 62.6 miles (100.8 km).

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the routes themselves, the area around Vic offers several points of interest. You can explore mountain peaks, visit historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5154658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Petroglyphs of the Pedra de l'Home&lt;/a&gt;, or see man-made monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5074387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gurb Cross&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5333496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Temple of Vic&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of a popular cycling loop.

Q: What is the total number of road cycling routes available in Vic?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Vic, offering a wide range of distances and difficulty levels to suit various preferences.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Montseny massif?
A: Yes, many routes in Vic offer stunning views of the Montseny massif. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995272074/taradell-from-vic-circular-route-with-views-of-montseny" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taradell from Vic – circular route with views of Montseny&lt;/a&gt; is specifically highlighted for its scenic vistas of this natural area.

Q: Can I find routes that include mountain passes?
A: Absolutely. Road cycling around Vic is defined by its mountain passes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15982793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Bracons – Coll de Condreu loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/16029820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Pullosa (917 m) – Collsuspina loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for cyclists who enjoy tackling significant ascents and descents through mountain terrain.
tips: The Plaza Mayor is the heart of the old quarter of the city of Vic. It is a monumental square, with a square plan and all porticoes. Here is the town hall, a Gothic building with a bell tower and several houses with beautiful facades of different architectural styles. In the center of the square there is a large unpaved space where the market takes place.
The town of Taradell is located 6 kilometers from Vic and is located at the foot of Montseny.
Flat and open, great for warming up the legs and enjoying the view!

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or less experienced riders?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered a moderate road ride and is suitable for cyclists of any fitness level. It's a great option if you're looking for a pleasant ride without overly demanding climbs.

faqs.question: What kind of road surface can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route largely consists of well-paved surfaces, which is typical for road cycling in the Montseny area. However, be aware that some segments might be unpaved, which could present a slight challenge for traditional road bikes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers panoramic views of Montseny. You'll pass through the historic Plaça Major de Vic, see the Taradell Monument, and the Sant Joan del Colomer Hermitage. A notable highlight is the View of Montseny from Malla, offering stunning vistas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montseny region, where this route is located, is particularly beautiful in autumn, with incredible landscapes. Generally, the Mediterranean climate makes it enjoyable for cycling for much of the year, but autumn offers unique scenery.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 26.1 kilometers long and typically takes around 1 hour and 14 minutes to complete by racebike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Taradell from Vic cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, as the route starts and ends in Vic, you would typically look for public parking options within Vic. Consider starting near the Plaça Major de Vic or other accessible areas in the town.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on public roads. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on leashes in public areas, it's generally not recommended to bring dogs on a racebike route for their safety and yours due to traffic and speed.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Tona - Vic (34%), Via Ausetana (34%), and Vic – Malla - Tona – Múnter – La Guixa – Vic (22%) trails.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the way?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Vic and passes through Taradell, both towns offer various options for cafes, restaurants, and other amenities where you can refuel or take a break.

faqs.question: What is the elevation gain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features an elevation gain of approximately 240 meters. This makes it a moderate ride, offering some gentle climbs but nothing overly strenuous, suitable for a relaxed cycling experience.
